channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Vejmelka makes 33 saves as Utah HC stays in playoff hunt
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Utah is not out of the playoff hunt just yet. Karel Vejmelka made 33 saves in his 18th consecutive start in goal, as the Utah Hockey Club stayed alive in the chase for the NHL playoffs with a hard-fought 3-1 victory over Calgary Tuesday night at the Delta Center.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Curry hits 12 3s and scores 51 as Warriors moving up in the West with a victory over the Grizzlies
item.description: The last time Stephen Curry and Golden State played in Memphis, he was held without a field goal and the Warriors lost by 51 points.
item.dc:creator: Clay Bailey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US Olympic and Paralympic officials fire coach and director after AP report on sexual abuse
item.description: The U.S. Olympic & Paralympic Committee has fired a coach and a director after The Associated Press reported that the coach was accused of sexually abusing a young biathlete.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: King fans 11 in 7-0 win against Guardians as the Padres are off to 1st 6-0 start
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Michael King struck out 11 in five innings and Jackson Merrill homered for the San Diego Padres, who started 6-0 for the first time in their 57-season history by beating the Cleveland Guardians 7-0 on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: BERNIE WILSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: NBA suspends 5 players for their roles in Pistons-Timberwolves altercation that spilled into stands
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Detroit's Isaiah Stewart was suspended for two games and four other players from the Pistons and Minnesota Timberwolves drew one-game suspensions for their roles in an on-court altercation between the teams earlier this week, the NBA said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jokes abound as Dodgers' Freddie Freeman misses 2nd game while recovering from shower slip and fall
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Freddie Freeman is hearing it after he slipped and fell in the shower, injuring his surgically repaired right ankle and missing his second straight game for the Los Angeles Dodgers. “Freak accident, you can’t really make it up, crazy,” Freeman said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ovechkin scores No. 891. He needs 3 to tie Gretzky and 4 to pass him
item.description: Alex Ovechkin moved four goals away from breaking Wayne Gretzky’s NHL record after scoring the 891st of his career in the Washington Capitals game against the Bruins in Boston on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Michigan State's Holloman, Booker, Michigan's Donaldson enter transfer portal, St. John's adds Sanon
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Michigan State's Tre Holloman plans to end his college career at another school and Xavier Booker is shooting for a fresh start. Holloman's agent, Brandon Grier, said the guard entered the transfer portal and informed coach Tom Izzo of his decision on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MIKE FITZPATRICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL owners green light several new rules for upcoming season, including kickoff and replay assist changes
item.description: NFL policy requires 24 votes from owners to approve any rule proposal. On Tuesday, several rules, including a change to the kickoff touchback, received the required votes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-Yankee player's son may have died of carbon monoxide poisoning
item.description: Miller Gardner, the 14-year-old son of former Yankees outfielder Brett Gardner, may have died from abnormal levels of carbon monoxide.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: ESPN's Stephen A Smith calls out LSU's Kim Mulkey after coach's exchange with reporter
item.description: ESPN star Stephen A. Smith described LSU women's basketball coach Kim Mulkey as "rude" after she had a terse back-and-forth with a reporter on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Carson Kelly becomes 1st Cubs player to hit for the cycle in 32 years
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — Carson Kelly had come close to hitting for the cycle, yet that elusive triple kept holding him back. He had just two triples over a 10-year big league career before Monday night. That's when Kelly became the first Chicago Cubs player in 32 years to hit for the cycle.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thunder rout Bulls 145-117 for 10th straight win, improve to 28-1 vs the East
item.description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 27 points and 12 assists in just 27 minutes, and the Oklahoma City Thunder rolled to a 145-117 victory over the Chicago Bulls on Monday night for their 10th straight victory.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jazz clinch worst record in franchise history
item.description: CHARLOTTE (ABC4 Sports) - It's official. The 2024-25 Utah Jazz will have the worst record in franchise history. Miles Bridges had 26 points, 10 rebounds and eight assists and the Charlotte Hornets defeated the Jazz 110-106 on Monday night hand Utah is 60th loss of the season.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: 2025 NASCAR Chicago Street Race: City, NASCAR announce traffic plan for before, during, after race weekend
item.description: CHICAGO — The City of Chicago and NASCAR officials have announced the traffic plan for the third NASCAR Chicago Street Race, scheduled for July 5-6 (Saturday and Sunday) in Grant Park.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Amalie Arena transforms for NCAA Women's Final Four
item.description: Amalie Arena is all about basketball for the next week, as the NCAA Women's Final Four takes over Tampa with events around the city.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Champions Retreat Golf Club ready to host Augusta National Women's Amateur following Hurricane Helene
item.description: The first practice round of the Augusta National Women's Amateur and other Masters related activities return to Georgia after Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Barclay Bishop, Fox 5

item.title: Houston's defense carries Cougars into 7th Final Four with 69-50 March Madness win over Tennessee
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Houston’s relentless, harassing defense had Tennessee's shooters dancing around. Now, the Cougars are two-stepping it back to Texas. L.J.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Duke's great D sends Blue Devils to their 18th Final Four with an 85-65 win over Alabama
item.description: Duke freshman Cooper Flagg swooped across the paint and swatted an Alabama player’s shot completely off the court. The ball landed in the stands. Flagg and the Blue Devils -- they’re headed to the Final Four.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr. rallies Florida into Final Four with 84-79 March Madness win over Texas Tech
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Coach Todd Golden cut the final strand of the net, waved it to the crowd and performed the famed Gator Chomp. A celebration that seemed completely improbable just a few minutes earlier was in high gear. Florida is heading back to the Final Four. Walter Clayton Jr.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Giddey's half-court buzzer beater completes miraculous comeback against Lakers, 119-117
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Josh Giddey could feel it as soon as the ball left his fingertips. In a flash, teammates were mobbing him.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sox fans shower Derrick Rose with MVP chants during first pitch
item.description: CHICAGO - Coming back to Chicago never gets old for Derrick Rose. The former Bulls MVP returned to the South Side Thursday to throw out the first pitch at the White Sox home opener, where he was showered with MVP chants from the crowd. "It's surreal. It's always surreal.
item.dc:creator: Rick Tarsitano, WGN 9

item.title: NCAA Tournament regionals: A look at Chicagoland connections, program history and more for the men's basketball Sweet 16
item.description: The 2025 NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament resumes Thursday night, and it's down to the Sweet 16. It'll be the Elite Eight after the regional semifinals on Thursday and Friday and then the Final Four after the regional finals on Saturday and Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Opening Day 2025: Cubs have a winning Opening Day record, featuring some memorable moments
item.description: CHICAGO — Opening Day is finally upon us. It's a day that comes with a sense of hope among baseball fans in early spring, a season that represents new beginnings often exemplified by the warmer temperatures and the blooming of trees and flowers.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WGN 9

item.title: White Sox Opening Day: A lot of 'new' on the South Side in 2025. What to know if you're headed to Rate Field
item.description: CHICAGO — After a 2024 season that everyone would rather forget, there's a lot of "new" on the South Side as the White Sox get set to embark on their 2025 campaign Thursday. There's a new manager, Will Venable. There's a new stadium name, Rate Field. There's a new food and beverage menu.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9
